title: ranunculus acris subsp. boreanus
intro: Ranunculus acris subsp. boreanus, also known as the alpine buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and narrow, lance-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be beneficial in treating wounds, skin irritations, and other health issues. The health benefits of Ranunculus acris subsp. boreanus include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health concerns. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent to create a medicinal extract.
